title: Circuit de Spa-Francorchamps
sub: The longest, fastest test on the calendar. Every corner, the sector splits, DRS zones and the Ardennes geography that makes Spa a drivers’ favourite.
mapCredit: Will Pittenger / Wikimedia Commons · CC BY-SA 3.0

sectors.name: Sector 1
sectors.char: La Source hairpin, the flat-out Eau Rouge/Raidillon launch and the long Kemmel Straight to Les Combes.

sectors.name: Sector 2
sectors.char: The long, twisting middle: Rivage, the mighty Pouhon, Fagnes and Campus. Downforce-limited and where laps swing.

sectors.name: Sector 3
sectors.char: Stavelot and Paul Frère feed the near-flat Blanchimont before the tight Bus Stop chicane onto the line.

tyreAlloc: One step softer · C2 / C3 / C4
stratNote: The 2024 resurface raised grip and degradation, tipping Spa firmly toward two stops. The long lap burns a lot of rubber, so the hard (C2) does heavy lifting.
spots: End of the Kemmel Straight after Eau Rouge + DRS 1. One of F1’s best passing spots.
Heavy braking off the main straight + DRS 2. Late-lunge territory.
Tight hairpin right after the line; switchbacks and opening-lap moves.
otNote: One of the easiest tracks to pass on. The FIA even shortened the Kemmel DRS zone in 2024 to stop overtakes being too easy. Slipstream effects are enormous.
corners: The slowest corner on the lap, a tight 2nd-gear right off the start straight.
Left-right-left compression, flat at 300+ km/h. The most famous sequence in motorsport.
Top of the Kemmel Straight. Heavy braking and the prime overtaking spot.
Fast left that flows out of Les Combes toward Bruxelles.
A tightening downhill right; the exit sets up the long run that follows.
Double right plunging downhill, hard on the front end.
A long, downhill double-apex left at ~290 km/h. One of F1’s great corners.
Right-left flick that breaks up the middle sector.
Slow right (a.k.a. Stavelot 1) leading toward the final sector.
Quick right at the far point, launching the long run up the hill.
Sweeping uphill right feeding the flat-out approach to Blanchimont.
Near-flat left at ~310 km/h. A real test of nerve.
Tight right-left chicane, the final braking zone and a late-lunge chance.
geo: Spa threads through the forested hills of Wallonia, with over 100m of elevation change. A rollercoaster as much as a racetrack.
The most famous sequence in motorsport: a compression that rockets uphill, taken flat at 300+ km/h.
At 7km, one sector can be soaked while another stays dry. Many great races (1998, 2008, 2021) were decided by rain.
Sectors 1 and 3 want low drag; the sector 2 sweeps want downforce. Teams pick a compromise wing level.
